summary: Justin Brennan conducts a live mentorship session analyzing two multifamily deals: a 9-unit property in North Carolina that needs price reduction to pencil (backing in from $1.7M to ~$1.35-1.4M), and a distressed 50-unit in Huntsville, Texas at 52% occupancy requiring bridge financing strategy. The session covers underwriting mechanics, valuation backing-in, and stressed asset acquisition approaches using bridge debt with interest reserves.
takeaways: Back into deals when metrics don't work: if asking $1.7M but deal only supports $1.35-1.4M, make aggressive LOI with follow-up phone call to broker saying 'I want to talk about the numbers' rather than radio silence
Target 15%+ IRR on value-add deals; in North Carolina 9-unit example, IRR was too rough and deal needed $300K-350K price reduction to work
For 2002-built property, use 30-40% expense ratio guidance; newer properties should be 30-35%, older properties closer to 40%
On distressed properties (50%+ vacancy), use bridge debt at ~9% interest rate for 2-3 years with interest reserve built into loan to carry until stabilization, then refinance
Maintain 2% gap between cap rate you build property to and market cap rate at exit (e.g., build to 8-9% cap, sell at 7% market cap)
Closing costs on smaller deals (9-30 units) should be 6%; drops to 4% on 80+ unit properties
Don't put money hard on deposits in current market (post-2022); keep all deposit money refundable through due diligence period
If missing expense data from wholesaler, use property age to estimate expense ratio range, then make aggressive offer and tell broker 'I need actual data to increase offer'

skill_candidates.change: Implement price-backing-in formula when IRR and cash-on-cash don't meet 15% IRR threshold
skill_candidates.evidence: Paula's 9-unit: offered $1.7M with 4.4% cash-on-cash and rough IRR; Justin backed into $1.35-1.4M to hit acceptable returns. Rule: 'if the numbers aren't there, you got to back in with price'

skill_candidates.change: Use expense ratio guidance by property age (30-40% range for 2002+ properties) to validate underwriting rather than building bottom-up
skill_candidates.evidence: 9-unit showed 36% expense ratio; Justin confirmed 'if you hover over this cell, it tells you 2010 property or newer should range between 30 to 40%' - gives rapid validation checkpoint

skill_candidates.change: Send aggressive LOI immediately followed by text/call to broker explaining position and requesting data conversation
skill_candidates.evidence: 'Even if it's a low offer, text them immediately after and say Hey just sent LOI it's pretty aggressive but I want to talk about numbers. That opens conversation versus if you just send low LOI you get middle finger'

skill_candidates.change: On distressed assets, calculate required cap rate gap (2%) between build-to cap and exit cap rather than targeting single cap rate
skill_candidates.evidence: Huntsville 50-unit: 'build it to 8-9 cap, then sell at market 7 cap and make money from 1-2% spread' - prevents overpaying by anchoring to market exit rate

title: Tuesday Mentorship Q&A - Build Deal Flow Systems -  01-27-26 | Justin Brennan
